LAW AND MOTION 302, DEFENDANT INTUITIVE SURGICAL, INC.'S DEMURRER TO PLAINTIFF'S COMPLAINT IS OVERRULED AS TO THE FIRST AND SEVENTH CAUSES OF ACTION, SUSTAINED WITH LEAVE TO AMEND AS TO THE FOURTH, SIXTH AND EIGHTH CAUSES OF ACTION, AND SUSTAINED WITHOUT LEAVE TO AMEND AS TO THE FIFTH CAUSE OF ACTION. THE LEARNED INTERMEDIARY DOCTRINE RAISES FACTUAL ISSUES NOT AMENABLE TO RESOLUTION ON DEMURRER. THE DEMURRER TO THE FOURTH CAUSE OF ACTION FOR BREACH OF THE IMPLIED WARRANTY IS SUSTAINED WITH LEAVE TO AMEND SO PLAINTIFF CAN ALLEGE PRIVITY BETWEEN THE PARTIES MORE CLEARLY. THE DEMURRER TO THE FIFTH CAUSE OF ACTION FOR BREACH OF THE EXPRESS WARRANTY IS SUSTAINED WITHOUT LEAVE TO AMEND AS UNOPPOSED. THE SIXTH AND EIGHT CAUSE OF ACTION FOR FRAUD AND NEGLIGENT MISREPRESENTATION ARE SUSTAINED WITH LEAVE TO AMEND TO PLEAD THEM WITH MORE PARTICULARITY. THE DEMURRER TO THE SEVENTH CAUSE OF ACTION FOR CONCEALMENT IS OVERRULED. THAT CLAIM REQUIRES LESS PARTICULARITY AND PLAINTIFF SUFFICIENTLY ALLEGES THAT DEFENDANT OWED A DUTY TO DISCLOSE BECAUSE OF DEFENDANT'S EXCLUSIVE KNOWLEDGE AND/OR PARTIAL SUPPRESSION. ORDER SIGNED IN OPEN COURT. JUDGE: HAROLD KAHN, REPORTER: LAVENA WARD, CSR #7077
ORDER RE DEMURRER TO PLAINTIFF'S COMPLAINT

DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT
Off Calendar Nov-01-2011 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
DEFENDANT INTUITIVE SURGICAL, INC.'S DEMURRER TO PLAINTIFF'S COMPLAINT.

APPEARANCES: Mitchell Shapson, Esq. 806-5514 specially appearing for pltfs & opposing parties; Diana Kotler, Esq. of Morris Polich & Purdy LLP 213/891-9100 for deft & moving party (appearing via CourtCall).

RULING: The Court adopted its tentative ruling - Overruled as to the first and seventh causes of action, sustained with leave to amend as to the fourth, sixth and eighth causes of action, and sustained without leave to amend as to the fifth cause of action. The learned intermediary doctrine raises factual issues not amenable to resolution on demurrer. The demurrer to the fourth cause of action for breach of the implied warranty is sustained with leave to amend so plaintiff can allege privity between the parties more clearly. The demurrer to the fifth cause of action for breach of the express warranty is sustained without leave to amend as unopposed. The sixth and eight cause of action for fraud and negligent misrepresentation are sustained with leave to amend to plead them with more particularity. The demurrer to the seventh cause of action for concealment is overruled. That claim requires less particularity and plaintiff sufficiently alleges that defendant owed a duty to disclose because of defendant's exclusive knowledge and/or partial suppression. Order signed in open court. Judge: Harold Kahn, Reporter: LaVena Ward, CSR #7077
